title: Information Theory for ML, by Hand
tagline: Entropy, cross-entropy, KL divergence and mutual information made visible — every quantity recomputed live as you drag distributions
summary: Drag a categorical distribution and watch entropy fall as it sharpens. Drop a model alongside data and see cross-entropy turn into KL plus a constant. Switch between forward and reverse KL while fitting a Gaussian to a bimodal target — and feel the difference between mode-covering and mode-seeking. Then build mutual information from a 2-D scatter and see why InfoNCE is a lower bound on it.
highlights: Drag a categorical distribution and watch entropy = expected surprise update live.
Cross-entropy and KL split visually: H(P,Q) = H(P) + KL(P‖Q).
Forward vs reverse KL on a fixed bimodal target — see mode-covering vs mode-seeking with one toggle.
Mutual information from a 2-D scatter; slide correlation; watch I(X;Y) shrink to 0 when independent.
InfoNCE bound on MI as the contrastive temperature shrinks.
